name: PureScript
description: A strongly-typed functional language inspired by Haskell that compiles to readable JavaScript

description.value: A strongly-typed functional language inspired by Haskell that compiles to readable JavaScript

initial-release.comment: Created by Phil Freeman in late 2013 as an alternative to transpiling Haskell to JavaScript

active-maintenance.comment: Regular releases and commits; PureScript Analyzer (Rust-based) emerging in 2025 for enhanced tooling

reactivity-model.comment: Halogen (primary UI library) uses a 100% PureScript virtual DOM implementation

component-model.comment: Components in Halogen are declarative functions with internal state and lifecycle

rendering-paradigm.comment: Halogen is a declarative, type-safe UI library

state-management-approach.comment: Halogen components have built-in state management via HalogenM monad

opinionatedness.comment: Halogen is unopinionated; supports Elm architecture or deep component trees

backward-compatibility.comment: PureScript maintains stability; compiler updates generally preserve backwards compatibility

primary-language.comment: PureScript is its own functional programming language, inspired by Haskell

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to readable JavaScript; experimental backends for C++, Go, Erlang

typescript-support.comment: PureScript has its own type system; TypeScript is not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Views written in PureScript using Halogen's HTML DSL (similar to Elm's approach)

styling-approach.comment: External CSS or inline styles via Halogen HTML attributes

build-required.comment: PureScript compiler and Spago build tool required

reactive-primitives.comment: Halogen provides reactive state via HalogenM; also purescript-signal available

computed-values.comment: Derived values via pure functions in render; computed from component state

two-way-binding.comment: No two-way binding; unidirectional data flow via actions and state updates

event-handling.comment: Declarative event handling in Halogen HTML (onClick, onInput, etc.)

conditional-rendering.comment: Via if/then/else and pattern matching in PureScript

list-rendering.comment: Via map functions over arrays/lists to generate HTML elements

component-slots.comment: Parent-child component communication with slots in Halogen

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal support in Halogen

suspense.comment: No Suspense; async operations handled via Aff monad and HalogenM

error-boundaries.comment: Type system prevents many runtime errors; no React-style error boundaries

context-provide-inject.comment: Can use ReaderT pattern or pass context through component tree

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Halogen provides RefLabel for direct DOM element access

official-router.comment: No official router; community libraries like purescript-routing and halogen-router available

router-name.comment: Community libraries; purescript-routing-duplex provides bidirectional parsing

route-guards.comment: Implementable in application logic before navigation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No built-in lazy route loading; requires external bundler configuration

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via FFI to JavaScript

built-in-state.comment: Halogen components have built-in state via HalogenM monad

built-in-global-state.comment: No built-in global state; typically use ReaderT or similar patterns

global-state-library.comment: No official global state library; patterns like ReaderT or custom stores used

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No dedicated PureScript devtools extension; browser DevTools with source maps available

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via FFI to localStorage

time-travel-debugging.comment: No built-in time-travel debugging; Halogen DevTools proposed but not available

official-cli.comment: Spago is the official PureScript package manager and build tool

hot-module-replacement.comment: Available via webpack configuration with PureScript loader

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated browser extension; Halogen DevTools proposed but not implemented

vs-code-extension.comment: PureScript IDE (nwolverson.ide-purescript) with language server support

error-messages.comment: Good error messages with type information; Haskell-inspired compiler output

documentation-quality.comment: PureScript by Example book, Halogen Guide, Pursuit package docs

bundle-size-core.comment: Hedwig (lightweight PureScript UI) ~11 kB minified+gzipped for Hello World

bundle-size-full.comment: Halogen apps typically 30-50 kB+ minified+gzipped depending on dependencies

virtual-dom.comment: Halogen uses a 100% PureScript virtual DOM implementation

compile-time-optimization.comment: PureScript compiler performs optimizations; dead code elimination available

tree-shaking.comment: Dead code elimination during compilation and bundling

code-splitting.comment: No built-in code splitting; requires external bundler configuration

ssr-capable.comment: Halogen SSR not officially supported; experimental hydration PR exists

js-framework-benchmark.comment: PureScript frameworks show ~2x slowdown compared to vanilla JS; design prioritizes safety over raw speed

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~1,230 weekly downloads for purescript npm package

major-companies-using.value: S&P Global, Standard Chartered, Zalando, JusPay, Awake Security (Arista)
major-companies-using.comment: Used in finance, payments, and security sectors; often for type-safe frontends

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven development; no major corporate sponsor

discord-community-size.comment: Active Discord server at purescript.org/chat; exact member count not published

job-market-demand.comment: Niche adoption; valued in finance and mission-critical applications but few job postings

official-testing-library.comment: purescript-spec and purescript-quickcheck from PureScript org

testing-library-name.comment: purescript-spec for BDD-style tests, purescript-quickcheck for property-based testing

component-testing.comment: Component testing via purescript-spec; DOM testing available

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress since output is standard JavaScript

mobile-framework.comment: Community bindings for React Native exist but not officially maintained

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; can be added manually

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop solution; can use Electron or Tauri

learning-curve.comment: Very steep; requires learning functional programming, monads, type classes, and new syntax

concepts-to-learn.comment: PureScript syntax, Types, Effects, Aff, Monads, Type Classes, Records, FFI, Halogen architecture, HalogenM, Routing, JSON decoding

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~30 minutes including tooling setup (Spago) and understanding basic syntax

migration-path.comment: Complete rewrite required; no migration tools from JS frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: PureScript can interop with JavaScript via FFI; can migrate one component at a time
